mappings.rationaleAI: Hinduism from the Indus Valley relates to Indian civilization (A), Indus civilization (B.2), caste system (B.3.b), South Indian development (B.7.b), and later developments (C, D.3).

The historical Buddha directly addresses the early development of Buddhism (B.4.a).

Alexander's conquest directly addresses the invasion of India (B.4.b).
Trade routes relate to South Indian maritime trade with the West (B.7.a).

Covers the Hellenistic world extending to the Indus, including Indo-Greek political fragmentation and multi-ethnic governance — directly relevant to Alexander's invasion and the rise of Indo-Greek and Asian rulers in small kingdoms of the north (150 BC-AD 300).

Traces the influence of Indian civilizations on Southeast Asian mandala kingdoms through Buddhism, Hinduism, and Confucianism — directly relevant to the character of traditional Indian civilizations and their influence on Southeast Asian civilizations.

Covers the Indus valley civilization spanning 4,000 years and the geographic and cultural territory of western Indian subcontinent — directly relevant to prehistoric settlements in the Indus Valley and Indus civilization development.
